revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:群馬縣護國神社]]


{{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017, 09:12 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name = Gunma Prefecture Gokoku Shrine
|image = [[File:Gunmaken Gokoku Shrine.JPG|280px]]
|location = 2000 Noritsuke-machi, [[Takasaki City]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}
|position = {{Coord|36|19|2.65|N|138|59|7.34|E|region:JP-10_scale:5000|display=it}}
|deity = National martyrs from Gunma Prefecture
|Rank = Minister of Home Affairs Designated Gokoku Shrine・Special Shrine
|founded = Shōwa 16 (1941)
|festival = {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|qid=Q2963|lt=October 16|1=October 16}}・{{ill|October 17|en|October 17|qid=Q2962|lt=October 17|1=October 17}}
|map = {{Switcher
 |{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|frame-height=180|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{Coord|36|19|2.65|N|138|59|7.34|E}}|frame-latitude=36.32|frame-longitude=138.996}}
 |Show Wikimedia maps
 |{{location map|Japan Gunma|float=center|width=220|label={{nowrap|Gunma Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}}}
 |Show Gunma Prefecture map
 }}
}}

'''Gunma Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (群馬県護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Takasaki City]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It enshrines 47,274 souls from Gunma Prefecture who died in wars from the [[Meiji Restoration]] to {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}.

== Overview ==
In Meiji 42 (1909), the Gunma Prefecture Shōkonkai was established, and annual memorial services were held at the Shrine of the Spirits in Takasaki Park. In Shōwa 14 (1939), following the system reform, it was decided to build Gokoku Shrines in each prefecture. On July 5, the Gunma Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Construction Committee was established. On November 8, Shōwa 16 (1941), it was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs, and on November 19 of the same year, the consecration ceremony was held, enshrining 3,573 souls who died in the Shina Incident. Since then, the shrine has enshrined war dead from the Greater East Asia War, now totaling 47,274 souls.

During the {{ill|GHQ|en|GHQ|qid=Q3756077|lt=GHQ|1=GHQ}} occupation after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was renamed to Seireibyō and later to Ueno Shrine. After Japan regained independence, the original name was restored on July 12, Shōwa 29 (1954).

On July 1, Heisei 8 (1996), the shrine was included in the [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]].

* An annual Gagaku concert, "Evening of Gagaku," is held for free in early May.
* In March Heisei 21 (2009), a statue of a Kamikaze warrior was erected.

== Wars and incidents commemorated ==
* Pre-Restoration and Restoration period (Genji 1 - Meiji 2)
* Satsuma Rebellion (Meiji 9)
* Seinan War (Meiji 10)
* First Sino-Japanese War (Meiji 27-28)
* Boxer Rebellion (Meiji 33-34)
* Russo-Japanese War (Meiji 37-38)
* Korean Rebellion Suppression Incident (Meiji 40-42)
* Taiwan Pacification (Meiji 29 - Taisho 3)
* Taisho Wars 3-9 (World War I, Siberian Intervention)
* Jinan Incident (Shōwa 3)
* Manchurian Incident (Shōwa 6-11)
* Shina Incident and Greater East Asia War

== External Links ==
* [http://www.gunmagokoku.info/index.html Gunma Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] (official site)
